refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the achievement of the customer-defined productivity target.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined two distinct aspects: enabling productivity achievement and supporting the measurement of that productivity. These are separated into atomic sub-FRs. The unit of measurement ('measured in m³/h or ton/h') is a detail of how productivity is measured, not what the system must fundamentally do to achieve it, making it a solution detail for the measurement aspect.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system's operational capabilities and features, when utilized, lead to the achievement of the specified productivity target under representative conditions.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1 (Productivity Achievement): The NGHE solution shall enable the achievement of the customer-defined productivity target.
FR-NGHE-001.2 (Productivity Measurement Support): The NGHE solution shall support the measurement of productivity.

candidate_dps: Optimize operational workflow and resource allocation
Implement real-time performance monitoring and feedback
Enhance operator skill and efficiency through training and tools
Minimize unplanned downtime through predictive maintenance and reliability engineering
selected_dp.selected_dp: Optimize operational workflow and resource allocation

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on a high-level logical solution concept that can be implemented with minimal inherent coupling to other DPs at this abstraction level. It provides a framework for achieving productivity without dictating specific technologies or tightly coupling to other functional areas.

assumptions_risks: Assumes that the customer-defined productivity target is achievable within the physical and operational constraints of the worksite.

Assumes that 'operational workflow and resource allocation' are the primary levers for achieving the productivity target, and that other factors (e.g., equipment capability, operator skill) are either sufficient or addressed by other means.
Risk: Inadequate data or models for workflow optimization could lead to suboptimal resource allocation.
Risk: Resistance to changes in workflow from operators or management could hinder adoption and impact productivity.
Risk: External factors (e.g., supply chain disruptions, weather) not accounted for in workflow optimization could impact productivity.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider refining the FR to specify the scope of 'productivity target' (e.g., per unit, per hour, per shift) if not already clear, to better inform DP selection and measurement. Also, consider if 'worksite safety plan and operational policies' introduce specific constraints on workflow optimization that need to be explicitly addressed in the DP.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The overarching system responsible for orchestrating and managing all operational aspects.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives operational data from various components and outputs performance metrics.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Issues commands and schedules to subordinate systems/modules.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communicates with external systems for data exchange and control.

module_containment_scores.notes: This DP is inherently high-level and cross-cutting, defining the overall system's operational strategy. The 'NGHE Solution' module, representing the entire system, is the most appropriate boundary for this strategic DP. It contains the core logic for workflow optimization and resource allocation across all sub-components.

The Vehicle module would only contain the execution of allocated resources and workflow steps, not the optimization or allocation logic itself. Significant diffusion would occur as the optimization logic needs to consider other system elements beyond just the vehicle.

Teleoperation is a mode of operation or a specific control mechanism, not the overarching logic for optimizing workflows and allocating resources across the entire system. It would only consume the results of such optimization.

Safety Services are a constraint or a specific function within the overall workflow, not the mechanism for optimizing the workflow or allocating resources. It would be a consumer or input to the optimization, not its container.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The DP 'Optimize operational workflow and resource allocation' is a strategic, system-level concern. It defines how the entire NGHE Solution operates efficiently. Therefore, the 'NGHE Solution' module, representing the top-level system, is the most cohesive boundary for this DP, as it encompasses the necessary scope to perform such optimization across all subordinate elements. Any narrower module would necessitate significant diffusion of the optimization logic.
containment_risks: The 'NGHE Solution' module is very broad. While appropriate for this high-level DP, it implies that the actual implementation of this optimization will likely involve sub-modules within 'NGHE Solution' that are not explicitly listed here. This could lead to a lack of specificity in subsequent decomposition if not managed carefully.
Risk of 'God Object' if too many high-level DPs are assigned to 'NGHE Solution' without further internal decomposition.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Optimize operational workflow and resource allocation' directly addresses the FR 'The NGHE solution shall enable the achievement of the customer-defined productivity target'. Optimizing workflow and resource allocation is a primary means to achieve a productivity target. The DP also implicitly considers 'Worksite safety plan and operational policies' as these would be constraints or inputs to any optimization process.

diffusion_risks: The 'NGHE Solution' module is very broad. While appropriate for this high-level DP, it implies that the actual implementation of this optimization will likely involve sub-modules within 'NGHE Solution' that are not explicitly listed here. This could lead to a lack of specificity in subsequent decomposition if not managed carefully.
Risk of 'God Object' if too many high-level DPs are assigned to 'NGHE Solution' without further internal decomposition.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The DP 'Optimize operational workflow and resource allocation' is a strategic, system-level concern. It defines how the entire NGHE Solution operates efficiently. Therefore, the 'NGHE Solution' module, representing the top-level system, is the most cohesive boundary for this DP, as it encompasses the necessary scope to perform such optimization across all subordinate elements. Any narrower module would necessitate significant diffusion of the optimization logic. The containment score of 9 for 'NGHE Solution' reflects this appropriate high-level mapping, while the low diffusion score of 2 indicates minimal scattering at this architectural level.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: The FR's acceptance criteria 'reported productivity is ≥ productivity_target' can be verified by the 'NGHE Solution' module through its 'Telemetry' interface (receiving operational data to calculate productivity) and its 'Tasking' interface (issuing commands that influence productivity). The 'Network Link' interface would support data exchange necessary for comprehensive productivity assessment and optimization.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The 'NGHE Solution' module, as the overarching system, is clearly allocated the responsibility for achieving the productivity target by optimizing workflow and resource allocation. This top-level allocation is clear, though it will require further decomposition within the 'NGHE Solution' to specify how this is achieved by sub-modules.

recommended_revisions: For subsequent decomposition, explicitly define the sub-modules within 'NGHE Solution' that will implement specific aspects of workflow optimization and resource allocation to mitigate the 'God Object' risk and provide more specificity.

Clarify how 'Worksite safety plan and operational policies' are integrated into the optimization logic within the 'NGHE Solution' module, potentially through specific sub-modules or configuration parameters.

decision.justification: All gates passed, indicating a sound mapping between the FR, DP, and IM at this level. However, the 'NGHE Solution' module is identified as very broad, with a risk of becoming a 'God Object' if further decomposition is not performed. To mitigate this and provide necessary specificity for a high safety criticality system, it is essential to derive sub-FRs that break down 'Optimize operational workflow and resource allocation' into more manageable and assignable functions within the 'NGHE Solution'. This will also help in explicitly integrating 'Worksite safety plan and operational policies' into the optimization logic.
next_level_sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall dynamically allocate resources (e.g., personnel, equipment) based on real-time operational data and predefined safety policies.
The NGHE solution shall generate optimized operational schedules that maximize productivity while adhering to worksite safety plans and regulatory compliance.
The NGHE solution shall monitor operational performance against targets and safety metrics, identifying deviations and recommending corrective actions.
The NGHE solution shall provide configurable parameters for integrating worksite safety plans and operational policies into workflow optimization algorithms.

assumptions: Real-time operational data will be available and sufficiently accurate for dynamic resource allocation and performance monitoring.
Worksite safety plans and operational policies can be formalized and integrated as constraints or objectives within optimization algorithms.
The NGHE solution will have the necessary interfaces to control and monitor resources for effective allocation and scheduling.
open_risks: Risk of 'God Object' if sub-modules within 'NGHE Solution' are not clearly defined and assigned specific responsibilities for workflow optimization and resource allocation.
Complexity of integrating diverse safety policies and operational constraints into a unified optimization model, potentially leading to sub-optimal solutions or safety compromises.
Performance overhead of real-time optimization and dynamic resource allocation, especially in high-throughput or rapidly changing operational environments.
evidence_needed: Detailed specification of data inputs required for optimization (e.g., resource availability, task status, environmental conditions).
Formalized representation of worksite safety plans and operational policies suitable for algorithmic integration.
Proof-of-concept or simulation results demonstrating the feasibility and effectiveness of dynamic resource allocation and optimized scheduling under various operational scenarios.
